title: EU-Morocco renew PRIMA agreement for Mediterranean research cooperation
content: The European Union and the Kingdom of Morocco have signed an agreement extending Morocco’s participation in PRIMA, the Partnership for Research and Innovation in the Mediterranean Area, for the 2025–2027 phase, with Morocco contributing €6.6 million over this period.

title: The transition to climate neutrality can go hand-in-hand with sustainable public finances
content: The transition to climate neutrality will significantly transform the EU economy but does not pose a threat to the sustainability of public finances, concludes a new study commissioned by the Commission’s Directorate-General for Climate Action (DG CLIMA).
snippet: The transition to climate neutrality will significantly transform the EU economy but does not pose a threat to the sustainability of public finances, concludes a new study commissioned by the Commissi

title: How the climate transition will impact the EU’s labour market: re-skilling and training needed
content: A new study, commissioned by the Directorate-General for Climate Action (DG CLIMA) assesses the impact of the transition to climate neutrality on the labour market, focusing on skills and occupations and their impact on the economy.
snippet: A new study, commissioned by the Directorate-General for Climate Action (DG CLIMA) assesses the impact of the transition to climate neutrality on the labour market, focusing on skills and occupations

title: Commission publishes new guidance to decarbonise buildings and increase their circularity
content: The document, which also aims to increase circularity, will support authorities and building professionals in their efforts to decarbonise buildings.
snippet: The document, which also aims to increase circularity, will support authorities and building professionals in their efforts to decarbonise buildings.

title: Commission and EU countries discuss latest developments concerning energy security
content: In the meetings of the Energy Union Task Force (EUTF) Security and the Oil Coordination Group held today, representatives from the European Commission and EU countries informed on the situation concerning the EU’s oil and gas security of supply, in view of the ongoing disruptions in the Middle East.

title: Report: Young peoples access to affordable and quality housing
content: A new report from the European Education and Culture Executive Agency sheds light on housing affordability challenges faced by young people across Europe.
snippet: A new report from the European Education and Culture Executive Agency sheds light on housing affordability challenges faced by young people across Europe.

title: Gas coordination Group confirms readiness to prepare for the upcoming winter season
content: In a meeting of the Gas Coordination Group held this morning, the Commission and EU countries discussed the EU’s security of gas supply in view of the ongoing disruption in the Middle East.
snippet: In a meeting of the Gas Coordination Group held this morning, the Commission and EU countries discussed the EU’s security of gas supply in view of the ongoing disruption in the Middle East.

title: Changes to support for people displaced from Ukraine in Poland
content: The Polish government drafted an official act to phase out measures introduced in 2022 to support people displaced from Ukraine by the ongoing war. Following a transitional period, beneficiaries of temporary protection would face reduced access to some family benefits, healthcare, and accommodation.

title: To Get Together project: promoting integration through culture in Slovakia
content: “To Get Together”, led by the Slovak Academy of Sciences with partners, supported people displaced from Ukraine by strengthening resilience and integration through safeguarding living cultural heritage.

title: EU check reveals misleading sales practices online
content: The European Commission and consumer protection authorities have discovered that some online discounts during Black Friday and Cyber Monday sales were misleading, thanks to the latest sweep.
snippet: The European Commission and consumer protection authorities have discovered that some online discounts during Black Friday and Cyber Monday sales were misleading, thanks to the latest sweep.

title: Commission welcomes historic agreement to reform EU Customs Union
content: The Commission welcomes the agreement between the European Parliament and the Council, delivering a landmark reform of the EU Customs Union. The most ambitious reform of EU customs rules since 1968 introduces new measures for e-commerce and launches a modern, data-driven customs architecture.

title: Rating scheme for data centres in the EU – Commission launches call for feedback
content: The European Commission has published a draft regulation shaping a common EU-wide rating scheme for data centres in the EU. It invites the public to provide feedback on the document over the next 4 weeks.

title: Borderless innovations: EU security event in Narva
content: On 5 and 6 March, CERIS co-organised a conference on „Borderless Border Management“ in Narva, Estonia, right on the Schengen external border with Russia. More than 200 attendees from 20 countries came together to discuss solutions that will directly impact the security of Europe and Estonia.

title: Strengthening CBRN Investigation, Prosecution and Adjudication Capabilities in the Western Balkans
content: As chemical, biological, radiological and nuclear (CBRN) crimes continue to pose complex security challenges, coordinated capacity-building efforts are essential to strengthen prevention, investigation and judicial response across regions.

title: Promoting student involvement at VET system level in the Netherlands
content: The EQAVET Dutch National Reference Point organised a peer review on the promotion of student involvement in quality assurance at VET system level, as students are considered a key stakeholder for education quality in the Netherlands and their participation in society is a policy priority.

title: Commission initiates safeguard investigation into imports of grain-oriented electrical steel
content: Today, the European Commission initiated an investigation to assess whether safeguard measures are needed for EU manufacturers of grain-oriented electrical sheets (GOES).
snippet: Today, the European Commission initiated an investigation to assess whether safeguard measures are needed for EU manufacturers of grain-oriented electrical sheets (GOES).

title: Juvenes Translatores contest: 2025-2026 winners celebrated in Brussels
content: 27 talented 17-year-olds from across the EU came to Brussels to collect their awards in the Juvenes Translatores school translation contest.
snippet: 27 talented 17-year-olds from across the EU came to Brussels to collect their awards in the Juvenes Translatores school translation contest.

title: Join the EUI Panel of Assessors for the European Urban Initiative - Innovative Actions Call 4

title: Gain the skills European employers need with vocational education and training
content: Jobseekers in today’s fast-changing EU market can learn the skills they need to take up a wide range of in-demand roles through vocational education and training (VET).
snippet: Jobseekers in today’s fast-changing EU market can learn the skills they need to take up a wide range of in-demand roles through vocational education and training (VET).

title: SAFE approvals unlock defence funding for Czechia and France
content: Following the approval of the first and second wave of defence funding, the European Commission has cleared two national defence plans under the Security Action for Europe (SAFE) initiative, further strengthening the Union's security and readiness. title: Commission scales up its humanitarian response in Lebanon to address urgent needs
content: This week, the Commission scaled up its humanitarian response in Lebanon, delivering nearly 150 tonnes of life-saving supplies to support people displaced by the deteriorating situation in the country.
snippet: This week, the Commission scaled up its humanitarian response in Lebanon, delivering nearly 150 tonnes of life-saving supplies to support people displaced by the deteriorating situation in the country

title: €115 million Programme for agile and rapid defence innovation (AGILE)
content: On 25 March 2026, The European Commission is presenting a new €115 million funding tool, AGILE, to bring disruptive defence technology from the lab to the field at record speed.
snippet: On 25 March 2026, The European Commission is presenting a new €115 million funding tool, AGILE, to bring disruptive defence technology from the lab to the field at record speed.

title: EU citizens show growing interest in electric vehicles, survey reveals
content: The European Alternative Fuel Observatory (EAFO) has published the Consumer Monitor 2025, offering new insights into consumer attitudes towards battery electric vehicles (BEVs) across the EU-27.
snippet: The European Alternative Fuel Observatory (EAFO) has published the Consumer Monitor 2025, offering new insights into consumer attitudes towards battery electric vehicles (BEVs) across the EU-27.

title: Commission and EU rail sector open nominations for Women in Rail Award 2026
content: This year’s Award will highlight individuals, teams and organisations that are driving change and creating concrete opportunities for women in the railway sector.
snippet: This year’s Award will highlight individuals, teams and organisations that are driving change and creating concrete opportunities for women in the railway sector.

title: EU boosts space cooperation with Norway and Iceland with Secure Connectivity Agreement
content: On March 26 2026 the European Union has signed an Agreement with Norway and Iceland allowing both countries to join GOVSATCOM and the Secure Connectivity Programme, IRIS². The Agreement highlights the EU’s commitment to cooperation with those countries.

title: EU-funded support for people displaced from Ukraine in Romania
content: Romania’s policy approach towards people displaced from Ukraine has progressively shifted from emergency humanitarian assistance to longer-term integration planning. In parallel with national measures, several EU-funded initiatives support structural integration in Romania.
